class Node:
    def __init__(self, val, prev=None, next=None, child=None):
        self.val = val
        self.prev = prev
        self.next = next
        self.child = child

def flatten(head: 'Node') -> 'Node':
    if not head:
        return None

    def _flatten_recursive(node: 'Node') -> 'Node':
        current = node
        # `last_processed_node` will track the actual tail of the list segment
        # being processed by this call, considering all children flattened.
        last_processed_node = node 

        while current:
            # Update `last_processed_node` to the current node before potentially moving `current`
            # or advancing to `current.next`. This ensures `last_processed_node` always holds
            # the last visited node in this segment.
            last_processed_node = current 

            if current.child:
                # Save the next node in the current level, as we're inserting a sublist
                next_node_at_current_level = current.next

                # Recursively flatten the child list and get its new tail.
                # `current.child` is the head of the child list.
                flattened_child_tail = _flatten_recursive(current.child)
                
                # Connect current node to the head of the flattened child list.
                current.next = current.child
                current.child.prev = current

                # Connect the tail of the flattened child list to the saved next node
                flattened_child_tail.next = next_node_at_current_level
                if next_node_at_current_level:
                    next_node_at_current_level.prev = flattened_child_tail
                
                # IMPORTANT: Set current.child to None after processing it
                current.child = None

                # After inserting the child list, the current pointer should logically
                # continue from the `flattened_child_tail`. This ensures the loop
                # continues from the end of the inserted sublist.
                current = flattened_child_tail
            
            # Move to the next node in the (potentially modified) list.
            # If `current` was just updated to `flattened_child_tail`, `current.next` will
            # correctly point to `next_node_at_current_level` (if not None).
            current = current.next
        
        # When the loop finishes, `last_processed_node` holds the last node of the
        # flattened segment that started from `node`.
        return last_processed_node

    # The main `flatten` function simply calls the helper on the `head`.
    # The list is modified in-place, so we return the original `head`.
    _flatten_recursive(head)
    return head
prompt: Flatten a multilevel doubly linked list yaz.
